temporal_description: A high-angle, wide shot captures a dimly lit interrogation room. Three men are seated around a large, dark table. On the far side of the table, a man in a white shirt and tie sits with a stern expression, looking down. Next to him, a man in a denim shirt looks down at his hands, appearing distressed. Across the table, a third man, seen from the back, gestures with his hands as if speaking. On the table are several briefcases, one open to reveal stacks of cash, and a notepad. The room is dark, with a cool, blueish light casting shadows across the wooden floor and the men. A watermark is visible in the top right corner. Chinese subtitles appear at the bottom of the screen. The audio is quiet and somber, with no background music, creating a tense and serious atmosphere. A man speaks in a low, deliberate voice, his tone filled with a sense of weary resignation and gravity. He begins, "爸爸常说" (Dad used to say), before pausing. The camera cuts to a close-up of the man in the white shirt. He is now looking down, his face partially obscured by shadow. The shot tightens further, focusing on the face of a man wearing glasses. He looks down with a somber and contemplative expression, his mouth slightly open as if speaking. The lighting remains dark and moody, highlighting the contours of his face. New Chinese subtitles appear at the bottom of the frame. He continues, "出来跑，无论做过什么..." (When you come out to run, no matter what you've done...). His slow, thoughtful pace and the quiet, almost hushed quality of his voice convey a deep sense of reflection and perhaps a touch of sadness or weariness. The camera remains in a tight close-up on the man with glasses. He lifts his head slightly, his expression shifting to one of serious determination. He looks forward, his eyes focused and intense. The subtitles at the bottom of the screen change. The watermark is still present in the top right corner. The shot is static, maintaining the intimate and serious mood of the scene.
